scope: Phase 1 showcase patient codes only. Covers conditions across Rhett759 (Synthea), the Cerner CCDA fixture, and synthesized artifacts. Phase 2 = full UMLS load (see BUILD-TRACKER D9).
notes: umls_cui values are taken from the UMLS 2024AA release via public concept search. Entries marked TBD were not confidently resolved at build time and require UMLS license access to verify.

codes.notes: Artifact 1 anchor — present in Cerner CCDA; merged with Synthea Condition via this CUI. SNOMED preferred term; ICD-10 I10 is the standard US billing code.
Present in Cerner CCDA; used in cross-vendor condition merge.
Active condition in Rhett759 Synthea record. ICD-10 R73.09 is commonly used when E11 not yet confirmed.
Active condition in Rhett759. Closely related to hypercholesterolemia (C0020443) in UMLS — see next entry.
Cerner CCDA uses hypercholesterolemia terminology; Synthea records hyperlipidemia. Both map via UMLS CUI grouping in Artifact 1 harmonization.

icd_10_cm.display: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease with (acute) exacerbation

codes.notes: Active condition in Rhett759. J44.1 represents exacerbation variant; J44.0 / J44.9 also plausible depending on encounter context.

icd_10_cm.display: Malignant neoplasm of upper lobe, bronchus or lung, unspecified side

codes.notes: Primary active malignancy for Rhett759. TNM stage 1 variant per Synthea. ICD-10 C34.10 used for stage-1 presentation without laterality specified.

icd_10_cm.display: Encounter for screening for malignant neoplasm of respiratory organs

codes.notes: Synthea records a 'suspected lung cancer (situation)' condition code prior to confirmed NSCLC diagnosis. Used for temporal ordering in harmonization.

snomed_ct.display: American Joint Commission on Cancer cT1 (qualifier value)

codes.notes: TNM staging qualifier. ICD-10 does not have a direct T1 staging code; clinical stage is conveyed via the primary C34.x code.
Active condition in Rhett759. Hemoglobin (LOINC 718-7) is the key lab indicator. Common comorbidity in lung cancer.

Artifact 4 planted free-text fact target. Phrase 'occasional chest tightness on exertion since approximately November of last year' in the synthesized SOAP note. Expected Layer 3 extraction → Condition (SNOMED 23924001).

Active medication in Rhett759 Synthea record. RxCUI 36567 is the ingredient-level concept. Artifact 2: Synthea shows simvastatin; Epic EHI projection shows atorvastatin — deliberate cross-source conflict.
Medication in the synthesized Epic EHI projection (Artifact 2 conflict). Both simvastatin and atorvastatin are HMG-CoA reductase inhibitors (statins) — clinically plausible cross-source discrepancy.
Active COPD/asthma inhaler for Rhett759 (Advair equivalent). RxCUI 896188 is a product-level concept; ingredient CUIs are fluticasone (C0083924) and salmeterol (C0074431).

Cerner CCDA records a generic 'statin' medication. During Artifact 2 medication reconciliation, this is used as the class anchor to detect conflict between simvastatin and atorvastatin. SNOMED code here points to atorvastatin as the CCDA-side specific substance when identified.
Artifact 5 anchor — LOINC 2160-0 maps to this SNOMED procedure concept. Used for PDF↔FHIR creatinine dedup via UMLS CUI bridge.
Broader synonym group for hyperlipidemia/hypercholesterolemia — used as fallback CUI if source uses 'dyslipidemia' terminology.
